name: Scan trends
description: Per-repo history of code-health scan results. scanTrends.ts appends one {t, total, active} point per completed scan and feature (dead-code, duplicates, type-escapes) to .code-workbench/scan-trends.json, capped at 60 points per feature. The scan pages' scan RPC returns the active-count series as `trend` (ScanPaneApi.scan optional field); ScanPanel renders it as a TrendSparkline (single 2px accent line, last-point dot, tooltip with first→last counts) in the tabs row. Trend persistence is best-effort — a write failure never fails the scan.
guidelines: Trend points record active (unacked) counts — that is the series users watch; total is kept for context.
The trend rides in the scan RPC result, not a separate event — keeps ScanPaneApi the single host contract.
Cap the series (MAX_POINTS) — the file is git-adjacent state and must stay small.
anti_patterns: Failing a scan because the trend write failed.
Adding axes/legend to the sparkline — it is a single-series glance, exact numbers live in the tooltip.
